findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed to source: `api_secret_key` has a default literal value (`"super-secret-key-do-not-share"`). Anyone with repo read access (or any downstream image / git history) gains the secret; with `pydantic-settings` the default is also used at runtime if `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is not set, so production would silently run on a public key. This contradicts the class docstring ("Never hardcode config.") and is a classic OWASP A02 (Cryptographic Failures) / A05 (Security Misconfiguration) finding.
findings.suggestion: Remove the default and require the value from the environment, e.g. `api_secret_key: SecretStr` (no default) so pydantic raises at startup if `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is missing. Load it from a secret manager / `.env` that is gitignored, rotate the leaked value, and purge it from git history.
